significance: Anthropic's current widely-available flagship, positioned for the most demanding long-horizon agentic and reasoning workloads, with always-on adaptive thinking and a 1M-token context window as the default. Generally available since June 9, 2026 across the Claude API, Claude Platform on AWS, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Foundry, alongside its Project-Glasswing-only sibling Claude Mythos 5. Anthropic's own documentation now points users needing the highest available capability to Fable 5 rather than the previously-flagship Claude Opus 4.8, making Fable 5 -- not Opus 4.8 -- Anthropic's most capable widely-released model as of this verification.

DeepSeek's newest flagship reasoning and agentic model extends default context to 1 million tokens and, per Lab DeepSeek, approaches the performance of leading closed frontier models while remaining open-weight and far cheaper to run via API.

Ai2 reports that the Olmo 3.1 update (an extended-RL refresh of the Olmo 3 family first released Nov 20, 2025) yields Ai2's strongest fully open 32B-scale reasoning and instruct checkpoints, with Ai2-reported gains of 5+ points on AIME and 20+ points on IFBench over the original Olmo 3 32B models.

NVIDIA's newest and largest Nemotron 3 release, Ultra is a 550-billion-parameter (55B active) Mixture-of-Experts hybrid Mamba-Transformer model built for long-running agentic workloads, and NVIDIA reports it reaches roughly 5x the inference throughput of comparable open models while matching their accuracy, including strong long-context retrieval at its 1M-token window.
